question: [File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.setConsumed(group, amount, victim) to advance victim's offer consumption to exactly the offer's maxUnits, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has an active offer with maxUnits capacity, triggering the call sequence operator grants consumptionMaxer via EcrecoverAuthorizer, consumptionMaxer calls Midnight.setConsumed(group, maxUnits, victim), fully consuming victim's offer without any actual fills, violating the invariant that offer consumption must reflect actual fills and not be settable to max by authorized delegates, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's offer capacity through unauthorized full consumption? Proof idea: unit test asserting consumptionMaxer can set victim's consumed to maxUnits and subsequent takes are no-ops.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization with authorization.nonce == nonce[victim] where the authorization grants authorization to a contract that will immediately revoke itself, under the precondition that victim has nonce=N and the attacker has obtained a signed authorization for this self-revoking grant, triggering the call sequence attacker submits Authorization{authorized=selfRevoker, isAuthorized=true, nonce=N} via EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized, consuming nonce N, then selfRevoker immediately calls Midnight.setIsAuthorized(selfRevoker, false, victim), leaving no trace of the authorization but consuming victim's nonce, violating the invariant that nonce consumption must correspond to a lasting authorization change, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's authorization nonce through ephemeral authorization grants? Proof idea: unit test asserting that a self-revoking authorization consumes victim's nonce without leaving any lasting authorization change.
